sanskrit: कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः। ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः॥
transliteration: kālo ’smi loka-kṣaya-kṛt pravṛddho lokān samāhartum iha pravṛttaḥ | ṛte ’pi tvāṃ na bhaviṣyanti sarve ye ’vasthitāḥ pratyanīkeṣu yodhāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Time am I, world-destroying, grown mighty, set forth here to annihilate the worlds; even without you, none of these warriors arrayed in opposing ranks shall survive.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reveals himself as the impersonal, inexorable flow of time that consumes every identity, role, and resistance.  Arjuna’s fantasy of preventing loss by withdrawing from action is shattered: the psyche’s defensive structures (the ‘armies’) are already slated for dissolution.  Fighting consciously becomes the act of cooperating with necessary ego-death rather than futilely postponing it.
psychological_state: Confrontation with irreversible transition; ego stripped of omnipotence; shift from anxious avoidance to purposeful engagement with unavoidable ending.
trigger_scenario: Receiving a terminal diagnosis for a loved one or oneself—realizing that no amount of negotiation, guilt, or withdrawal will halt the outcome, and that meaningful action must now occur within the remaining time.
anchor_text: Surrender to time's flow; fight as the witness, not the victim.
